A. tote
B. coffer

:';
MMddyyyy'}),('2023-10-05 14:30:00','Pacific Standard Time',CONVERT(datetime2, '2023-10-05 14:30:00', 126)),('2023-10-05 15:00:00','Eastern Standard Time',CONVERT(datetime2, '2023-10-05 15:00:00', 128)),('2023-10-05 15:30:00','Central Standard Time',CONVERT(datetime2, '2023-10-05 15:30:00', 130)),('2023-10-05 16:00:00','Mountain Standard Time',CONVERT(datetime2, '2023-10-05 16:00:00', 132))])

-- Query to find the latest timestamp for each timezone
SELECT TimeZoneID, MAX(TimestampUTC)
FROM TimezoneData
GROUP BY TimeZoneID;

-- Query to convert UTC timestamps to local timestamps based on the latest conversion rule
WITH LatestConversions AS (
    SELECT TimeZoneID, MAX(TimestampUTC) AS LatestTimestamp
    FROM TimezoneData
    GROUP BY TimeZoneID
)
SELECT td.TimeZoneID, td.TimestampUTC, lc.LatestTimestamp,
       CASE 
           WHEN td.TimeZoneID = 'Pacific Standard Time' THEN DATEADD(hour, DATEDIFF(hour, '2023-10-05 00:00:00', td.TimestampUTC), lc.LatestTimestamp)
           WHEN td.TimeZoneID = 'Eastern Standard Time' THEN DATEADD(hour, DATEDIFF(hour, '2023-10-05 00:00:00', td.TimestampUTC), lc.LatestTimestamp)
           WHEN td.TimeZoneID = 'Central Standard Time' THEN DATEADD(hour, DATEDIFF(hour, '2023-10-05 00:00:00', td.TimestampUTC), lc.LatestTimestamp)
           WHEN td.TimeZoneID = 'Mountain Standard Time' THEN DATEADD(hour, DATEDIFF(hour, '2023-10-05 00:00:00', td.TimestampUTC), lc.LatestTimestamp)
       END AS LocalTimestamp
FROM TimezoneData td
JOIN LatestConversions lc ON td.TimeZoneID = lc.TimeZoneID;
```

This SQL script sets up a table with timezone data and provides queries to find the latest UTC timestamp for each timezone and then converts those UTC timestamps to local timestamps based on the latest conversion rules provided. Adjust the `TimeZoneID` values as needed for your specific use case.
